scope_note: QA Pisano All-Initializations Cert [499]. CLAIM (narrow): QA three-orbit partition of {1,...,9}^2 under sigma(b,e)=(e,((b+e-1)%9)+1) — Cosmos 72 pairs period 24, Satellite 8 pairs period 8, Singularity 1 pair (9,9) period 1, total 72+8+1=81=9^2 — is structurally equivalent to Pudelko's (arXiv:2510.24882 v5 2026-04-09) complete Pisano period classification of ALL m^2=81 initial pairs in (Z/9Z)^2 finding the same three periods {1,8,24} with the same counts. Additional checks: (QAP_3) swap (b,e)->(e,b) preserves orbit family for all 81 pairs; (QAP_5) additive negation (b,e)->(neg(b),neg(e)) preserves orbit family for all 81 pairs, mirroring Pudelko's parity observation. Content-ideal interpretation: Cosmos = min(v_3(b),v_3(e))=0, Satellite = min=1, Singularity = min=2 (from companion cert [261] qa_orbit_stratification_cert_v1). NOT CLAIMED: full Pudelko distribution P(n)=P(1-n), fractal prime-power structure beyond m=9, or continuous-time modeling. Checks QAP_1/QAP_2/QAP_3/QAP_4/QAP_5/SRC/F; 1 PASS + 3 FAIL fixtures; self-test ok. Primary sources: Pudelko M.T. (2025) arXiv:2510.24882 doi pending; Wall D.D. (1960) doi:10.2307/2309169; Wildberger N.J. (2005) ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8. Companion: cert [128] qa_fibonacci_matrix_orbit_periods_cert_v1 (Lean proof pi(9)=24); cert [261] qa_orbit_stratification_cert_v1 (content-ideal classification). Derived 2026-06-23.
